target.name: Artimus Robotics
target.founder: Eric Acome, Shane Mitchell, Nick Kellaris, Christoph Keplinger

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Artimus Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Eric Acome, Shane Mitchell, Nick Kellaris, and Christoph Keplinger, having raised $5M to date. The target operates within a sector peer set of 9 private robotics companies and 1 public comparable, including notable private entrants such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Valuation analysis relied on a single applicable methodology due to data constraints. The Stage-Progression method applied a 2.5x multiplier to the $5M total raised proxy, reflecting typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up dynamics in early-stage venture capital. This yielded an implied valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, insufficient granular comparables met narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-valuation-tier requirements of ≥3 datapoints each), making sector-wide median extrapolation unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of tight-peer multiples. Refinement of this estimate would benefit from either a larger set of same-stage robotics comparables or additional transaction precedent within the frontier hardware space.
